title: 10 best herbal capsules for gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gastritis are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs and natural ingredients, designed to alleviate the symptoms of gastritis. Gastritis is an inflammation of the stomach lining, often caused by bacterial infections, stress, or the prolonged use of certain medications. Herbal capsules for gastritis can help reduce inflammation, neutralize stomach acid, and promote healing of the stomach lining. Examples of herbs commonly found in these capsules include licorice root, slippery elm, marshmallow root, chamomile, and turmeric. By taking herbal capsules for gastritis, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, indigestion, and nausea, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort capsule helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and kill harmful bacteria, such as Helicobacter pylori, which is a common cause of gastritis. Additionally, mugwort has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, promoting proper digestion and reducing the symptoms of gastritis, such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Furthermore, mugwort can help strengthen the stomach lining, providing protection against future gastritis attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is a condition characterized by inflammation of the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: Mugwort capsules can inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, particularly thujone and camphone.

Protective: Mugwort capsules can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid and other irritants, which is important for gastritis as it can help prevent further irritation and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and tannins.

Analgesic: Mugwort capsules can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, particularly thujone and 1,8-cineole.

Spasmolytic: Mugwort capsules can relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping, which is important for gastritis as it can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and bloating. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, particularly flavonoids and alkaloids.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the mugwort plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help soothe and heal the stomach lining.
Leaves: The leaves of the mugwort plant are used in medicinal tea for gastritis as they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that can help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.

Flowers: The flowers of the mugwort plant are sometimes included in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their calming and sedative effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the mugwort plant are used in medicinal tea for gastritis because they are rich in antioxidants and can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried mugwort herb and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground mugwort powder with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch or another natural binder in a small bowl.
Gradually add just enough water to create a thick, pliable dough, kneading it gently in the bowl.
Divide the dough into 10 equal-sized pieces, roll each piece into a small ball, then flatten them into capsule shapes.
Dry the mugwort capsules at room temperature for 24-48 hours, then store in an airtight container for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with gastritis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Gastritis is often caused by an H. pylori bacterial infection or inflammation, and barberry's active compounds can help reduce both. Barberry may also help to heal the stomach lining and reduce the production of excess stomach acid, providing relief from gastritis symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Furthermore, it can stimulate the production of bile, which aids in digestion and the absorption of nutrients, promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Barberry contains berberine, which helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, a key factor in gastritis.
Antimicrobial: The berberine in barberry has antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the H. pylori bacteria often associated with gastritis.
Astringent: Barberry's tannins help to tighten and protect the mucous membranes of the stomach, reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Protects gastric mucosa: Barberry's alkaloids, such as berberine, have a protective effect on the gastric mucosa, helping to prevent and heal gastritis.
Regulates gastric acid: Barberry can help regulate gastric acid secretion, providing relief from gastritis symptoms caused by excessive acid production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the common barberry plant contain berberine, which is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce gastritis symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the barberry plant is rich in alkaloids, which are believed to have a protective effect on the stomach lining and may help reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.
Berries: The berries of the barberry plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce damage to the stomach lining caused by free radicals and may help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000mg of dried common barberry bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 50mg of a binding agent, such as microcrystalline cellulose, in a bowl.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry location, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with gastritis because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and soothe digestion. The polyphenols in white tea have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, such as H. pylori, which is a common cause of gastritis. Additionally, white tea has been found to help improve the gut microbiome, promoting a healthy balance of bacteria in the stomach and reducing the risk of gastritis. White tea capsules may also help to strengthen the stomach lining, providing further protection against gastritis. Overall, white tea capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage gastritis symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms. This property is important for gastritis because inflammation is a key factor in the development and persistence of gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include catechins, such as EGCG, and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of gastritis. This property is important for gastritis because oxidative stress can contribute to the development and progression of the condition. The primary antioxidants in white tea are flavonoids, including catechins and epicatechins.

Antibacterial: White tea exhibits antibacterial properties, particularly against Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium strongly linked to gastritis. This property is important for gastritis because H. pylori infection is a common cause of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols, such as catechins and tannins, which can inhibit H. pylori growth and activity.

Gastroprotective: White tea has gastroprotective properties that help maintain the stomach lining's integrity, preventing damage and promoting healing. This property is important for gastritis because it can help alleviate symptoms and support the recovery of the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as catechins and epicatechins, which can increase mucus secretion and enhance the stomach lining's resistance to damage.

Anti-ulcer: White tea has been shown to have anti-ulcer properties, which can help prevent and heal stomach ulcers associated with gastritis. This property is important for gastritis because ulcers can be a complication of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as catechins and epicatechins, which can inhibit gastric acid secretion and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gastric inflammation.
Buds: Rich in polyphenols and catechins, which have been shown to protect the stomach lining and reduce gastritis symptoms.
Flowers: Have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated stomach tissue and alleviate pain.
Stems: Contain tannins and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation, prevent the growth of harmful bacteria, and promote healing of gastric ulcers.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce gastritis symptoms, prevent infections, and promote overall stomach health.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried herbs, including 2 teaspoons of chamomile flowers, 2 teaspoons of peppermint leaves, 1 teaspoon of licorice root, and 1 teaspoon of slippery elm bark.
Combine herbs in a heat-resistant bowl, then pour 2 cups of boiling water over the mixture, allowing it to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the herbal mixture, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible; compost the solids.
Measure 1/2 cup of the cooled herbal liquid, then combine it with 1/2 cup of a binders such as tapioca starch or potato starch in a small mixing bowl.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal-starch mixture using a capsule filling machine, then let the capsules dry for 24 hours before storing them in airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with gastritis because it contains caraway oil, which has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive disorders. The oil's active components, such as carvone and limonene, have been found to have antispasmodic, carminative, and antibacterial properties. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as abdominal pain, bloating, and excess gas, by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach. Moreover, caraway oil can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting proper digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can further prevent the onset of gastritis. Therefore, taking caraway capsules can provide a natural and effective way to manage gastritis and maintain a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Caraway capsules help to expel gas from the stomach, which is important for gastritis as it can help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The active constituents that give caraway its carminative properties are the volatile oils, particularly carvone and limonene.

Anti-inflammatory: Caraway capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, a key characteristic of gastritis. The active constituents that contribute to caraway's anti-inflammatory properties include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antispasmodic: Caraway capsules can help to relax the smooth muscle of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping, which are common symptoms of gastritis. The active constituents responsible for caraway's antispasmodic properties include the volatile oils and flavonoids.

Protective: Caraway capsules have been shown to have a protective effect on the gastric mucosa, which can help to prevent the damage caused by gastritis. The active constituents that contribute to caraway's protective properties include tannins and rosmarinic acid.

Digestive stimulant: Caraway capsules can help to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and absorption, and may help to reduce the symptoms of gastritis. The active constituents that give caraway its digestive stimulant properties include the volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway plant are used in tea to soothe digestive issues, including gastritis, due to their anti-inflammatory and calming effects on the stomach lining.
Rhyzomes: Caraway rhyzomes are rich in essential oils and have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating gastritis.
Flowers: The flowers of the caraway plant are used in tea for their mild sedative effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried caraway seeds and 1 cup of water.
Grind caraway seeds into a fine powder, then mix with 1 cup of gelatin or plant-based capsule filler.
Fill size 00 empty capsules with the caraway seed and filler mixture, using a capsule-making machine or by hand.
Allow capsules to dry for 24 hours at room temperature.
Store completed caraway seed capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common centaury capsule helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms. This herb has been traditionally used to treat digestive disorders, including gastritis, due to its ability to stimulate the production of digestive juices and protect the mucous membrane of the stomach. Moreover, common centaury has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help to tone and strengthen the stomach lining, further reducing the risk of gastritis. Overall, common centaury capsule can provide a natural and effective solution for managing gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Centaury has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms. This is due to the presence of bitter compounds like gentianin and xanthones.
Astringent: Centaury acts as an astringent, helping to tighten and tone the mucous membranes of the stomach, protecting them from irritation and damage. This is attributed to its tannin content.
Choleretic: Centaury stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach. This is due to the presence of bitter compounds like gentianin.

Antispasmodic: Centaury has antispasmodic properties that help relieve muscle spasms and relax the stomach, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. This is attributed to its volatile oil content.

Antimicrobial: Centaury has antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach, reducing the risk of infection and further irritation. This is due to the presence of flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their bitter properties that can help stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining.
Flowers: The flowers of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal the stomach lining.
Stems: The stems of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach.

Roots: The roots of the common centaury capsule plant are not commonly used in medicinal tea for gastritis, as they contain higher levels of bitter compounds that can be too harsh on the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried common centaury herb and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the ground herb with 1-2 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as gelatin or plant-based gum, to help hold the capsule together.
Load the herb-binding agent mixture into empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24-48 hours in a cool, dark place to ensure the binding agent sets properly.
Store the finished centaury capsules in an airtight container, away from direct sunlight, and take as directed by a healthcare professional for gastritis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with gastritis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, one of the primary causes of gastritis. Additionally, curcumin can help increase mucus secretion, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. Furthermore, turmeric has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with gastritis. By addressing these underlying factors, turmeric capsules can provide relief from gastritis symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is often caused by inflammation. This property is due to the active constituent curcumin.

Antioxidant: Turmeric has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage, which is beneficial for gastritis as it can be caused by oxidative stress. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Antibacterial: Turmeric capsules have antibacterial properties that can help kill H. pylori, a bacterium often associated with gastritis. Curcumin and other compounds in turmeric, such as turmerone, exhibit this property.

Gastroprotective: Turmeric can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by alcohol, nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), and other irritants, making it helpful for gastritis. The gastroprotective effect is attributed to curcumin and other turmeric constituents.

Pain-relieving: Turmeric has analgesic properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. This property is also due to curcumin, which inhibits the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that contribute to pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem or rhizome is used for making medicinal tea for gastritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and heal the stomach lining.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis because they contain curcumin, which has been shown to help reduce inflammation in the stomach and improve digestion.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach and promote a healthy gut.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis due to their ability to stimulate the production of bile, which can improve digestion and help prevent gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: 1 cup of turmeric powder, 1/2 cup of ginger powder, 1/4 cup of black pepper, and 1/4 cup of baking soda.
Combine all ingredients in a large mixing bowl, mixing thoroughly for 10 minutes.
Using a capsule-filling machine, fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, setting aside any excess.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours at room temperature.
Store the finished herbal turmeric capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with gastritis because it contains active compounds that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining. The main active ingredient in licorice, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to increase the mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach. Additionally, licorice has been found to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium that is often associated with gastritis. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of H. pylori, licorice capsules can help to alleviate the symptoms of gastritis, such as pain, bloating, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a primary cause of gastritis, due to the presence of glycyrrhizic acid.
Mucosal protective: It helps protect and heal the stomach lining by increasing the mucus secretion, which is beneficial for gastritis, because of the flavonoids and coumarins.
Antacid: Licorice has the ability to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to the saponins and triterpenoids.
Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium commonly linked to gastritis, by the presence of flavonoids and coumarins.
Gastroprotective: Licorice stimulates the regeneration of stomach lining cells, promoting healing and preventing the recurrence of gastritis, due to the glycyrrhizic acid and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the stomach, making it effective for treating gastritis.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice capsule plant contain various flavonoids and coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for soothing and healing the stomach in cases of gastritis.

Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant contain various essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-spasmodic and carminative properties, making it effective for relieving symptoms of gastritis such as pain, bloating, and flatulence.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2000mg of dried licorice root and a capsule filling machine.
Grind the licorice root into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Load the powdered licorice root into the capsule filling machine, and compress it into gelatin capsules.
Ensure each capsule contains 500mg of licorice root powder.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, and take as directed by a healthcare professional for gastritis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Immortelle capsule helps with gastritis because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called chamazulene, which helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Additionally, immortelle has been shown to stimulate the production of gastric mucus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. Its antispasmodic properties also help alleviate abdominal pain and cramping associated with gastritis. Furthermore, immortelle has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly linked to gastritis. By addressing these underlying causes, immortelle capsules can provide relief from gastritis symptoms and support overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Immuortelle's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to the presence of compounds like chamazulene and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of immortelle helps protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, supporting gastritis healing, owing to its high content of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Immuortelle's antimicrobial property aids in inhibiting the growth of H. pylori, a primary cause of gastritis, by leveraging its active constituents such as α-pinene, sabinene, and 1,8-cineole.

Gastroprotective: The gastroprotective property of immortelle helps safeguard the stomach lining from damage and promotes healing in gastritis, attributed to its rich supply of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Digestive: Immuortelle's digestive property supports healthy digestion and reduces gastric irritation, offering relief for gastritis, due to the presence of essential oils and bitter compounds.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, immortelle flowers help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, immortelle leaves soothe gastritis symptoms.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, immortelle buds aid in digestion and alleviate gastritis discomfort.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, immortelle seeds support gut health and help treat gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 teaspoons of dried immortelle flowers, 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves, and 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers in a mixing bowl.
Transfer the herbal mixture to a mortar and pestle, then gently crush the herbs for 2-3 minutes to release their essential oils.
Place a tablespoon of the crushed herbs into a capsule-making machine, followed by a second empty capsule half; press down firmly to fill and seal the capsule.
Repeat step 3 until all the herbal mixture is used, creating multiple herbal immortelle capsules for gastritis treatment.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry, and airtight container for up to 6 months, taking 1-2 capsules daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile capsule helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. The herb's active compounds, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, can help soothe the stomach, alleviate pain, and promote digestion. German chamomile is also known for its calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms. Furthermore, its antibacterial properties may help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to gastritis. Overall, German chamomile capsules can provide a natural and effective way to manage gastritis symptoms and support overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it is primarily characterized by inflammation. This property is attributed to the active constituents, including the flavonoid apigenin and various sesquiterpenes like alpha-bisabolol.

Antispasmodic: German chamomile can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps. This property is crucial for gastritis as it alleviates pain and discomfort caused by stomach spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property include the terpenoids chamazulene and bisabolol.

Antioxidant: German chamomile has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for gastritis as it can help prevent further damage and promote healing. The antioxidant properties come from various flavonoids and terpenoids present in German chamomile.

Antibacterial: German chamomile has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with gastritis. This property is significant for gastritis as it targets one of the primary causes of the condition. The antibacterial properties are attributed to the essential oils and flavonoids found in German chamomile.

Mucosal protective: German chamomile can help create a protective barrier on the stomach lining, reducing its exposure to harmful substances. This property is essential for gastritis as it can help prevent further irritation and damage to the stomach lining. The mucosal protective effect is attributed to the flavonoids and terpenoids present in German chamomile.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the stomach lining and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation, promote digestion, and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried German chamomile flowers and size “00” empty capsules.
Measure 2-3 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers and grind them into a fine powder.
Fill each empty capsule with the ground chamomile powder using a capsule filling machine.
Store the finished chamomile capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to six months.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, as a natural remedy for gastritis. Consult a healthcare professional before use if pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking other medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsule helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which may help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. Furthermore, lemon balm may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to the development and exacerbation of gastritis. By promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and fighting bacteria, lemon balm capsules can provide relief and support for individuals suffering from gastritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastric inflammation, a primary cause of gastritis, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effects of lemon balm alleviate abdominal pain and cramping associated with gastritis by relaxing gastrointestinal muscles, thanks to its volatile oils and tannins.
Carminative: Lemon balm's carminative properties help relieve gas and bloating in gastritis patients by promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas, owing to its essential oils and terpenes.
Anxiolytic: The anxiolytic effects of lemon balm can help manage stress-induced gastritis by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Antimicrobial: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help combat Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium linked to gastritis, due to its citral, geraniol, and linalool constituents.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for gastritis.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves and flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain beneficial compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote digestive health, making them a suitable addition to medicinal tea for gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, rinse, and let them air dry.
Grind the dried lemon balm leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 500mg of the lemon balm powder and mix it with an appropriate filler like microcrystalline cellulose to make it easier to fill the capsule shells.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the lemon balm-filler mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the finished lemon balm capsules in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times a day as needed for gastritis symptoms.
